label: Geometry
shortDescription: Euclidean geometry, proofs, congruence, similarity, circles, and spatial reasoning.
modulesDescription: Read the geometry chapters in order, or jump directly to the topic you need.
ogDescription: Master Euclidean geometry, triangle congruence, circles, coordinate geometry, transformations, and an introduction to projective and non-Euclidean geometries — with detailed explanations, worked examples, and practice questions.
courseDescription: A complete geometry course covering foundations (points, lines, planes, axioms), triangle congruence and similarity, Pythagorean theorem, circles and polygons, coordinate methods, isometries and symmetry, and non-Euclidean geometries. Features ELI5 intuition, step-by-step worked examples, common mistakes, and hundreds of practice problems.

topics.title: Geometry
topics.description: Introduction to the basic concepts and scope of geometry as the study of shapes, sizes, and properties of space.

topics.title: Euclidean geometry
topics.description: The classical system of geometry based on Euclid's axioms and postulates, foundation of plane geometry.

topics.title: Point (geometry)
topics.description: The most basic undefined element in geometry: an exact location with no size or dimension.

topics.title: Line (geometry)
topics.description: A straight one-dimensional figure extending infinitely in both directions, defined by two points.

topics.title: Plane (geometry)
topics.description: A flat two-dimensional surface extending infinitely, the setting for Euclidean plane geometry.

topics.title: Angle
topics.description: The figure formed by two rays sharing a common endpoint, measured in degrees or radians.

topics.title: Axiom
topics.description: A statement accepted as true without proof, serving as the starting point for geometric reasoning.

topics.title: Euclid's Elements
topics.description: The foundational treatise by Euclid compiling definitions, postulates, and propositions of geometry.

topics.title: Parallel postulate
topics.description: Euclid's fifth postulate about parallel lines and the key to distinguishing Euclidean from non-Euclidean geometry.

topics.title: Triangle
topics.description: A closed figure with three straight sides and three angles, the simplest polygon.

topics.title: Congruence (geometry)
topics.description: When two figures have the same size and shape, corresponding parts equal via rigid motions.

topics.title: SSS congruence
topics.description: The side-side-side criterion: three sides determine a unique triangle up to congruence.

topics.title: SAS congruence
topics.description: The side-angle-side criterion: two sides and the included angle determine congruence.

topics.title: ASA congruence
topics.description: The angle-side-angle criterion: two angles and the included side determine congruence.

topics.title: Pythagorean theorem
topics.description: In a right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of squares of the other two sides.

topics.title: Similarity (geometry)
topics.description: Figures that have the same shape but possibly different size; corresponding angles equal, sides proportional.

topics.title: AA similarity
topics.description: The angle-angle criterion: two pairs of equal angles imply similar triangles.

topics.title: Isosceles triangle theorem
topics.description: Base angles of an isosceles triangle are equal; converse also holds.

topics.title: Triangle inequality
topics.description: The sum of any two sides of a triangle must be greater than the third side.

topics.title: Circle
topics.description: The set of all points in a plane at a fixed distance (radius) from a center point.

topics.title: Radius
topics.description: The distance from the center of a circle to any point on its circumference.

topics.title: Diameter
topics.description: A chord passing through the center of a circle; twice the radius, the longest chord.

topics.title: Polygon
topics.description: A closed plane figure bounded by straight line segments.

topics.title: Regular polygon
topics.description: A polygon with all sides and all interior angles equal.

topics.title: Inscribed angle
topics.description: An angle whose vertex lies on a circle and whose sides pass through two other points on the circle.

topics.title: Circumscribed circle
topics.description: The unique circle that passes through all vertices of a polygon (circumcircle).

topics.title: Area of a circle
topics.description: The measure of the region enclosed by a circle; formula A = πr² derived via limits or sectors.

topics.title: Cartesian coordinate system
topics.description: A coordinate plane formed by two perpendicular axes allowing algebraic representation of geometric objects.

topics.title: Distance formula
topics.description: The formula √[(x_{2}−x_{1})^{2} + (y_{2}−y_{1})^{2}] giving distance between two points in the plane.

topics.title: Midpoint
topics.description: The point exactly halfway between two endpoints; average of coordinates.

topics.title: Slope
topics.description: The measure of steepness of a line: rise over run, or (y_{2}−y_{1})/(x_{2}−x_{1}).

topics.title: Equation of a line
topics.description: Algebraic equations such as y = mx + b or ax + by + c = 0 representing straight lines.

topics.title: Equation of a circle
topics.description: The equation (x−h)^{2} + (y−k)^{2} = r^{2} describing all points at distance r from center (h,k).

topics.title: Parabola
topics.description: The curve formed as the set of points equidistant from a focus and a directrix; quadratic graph.

topics.title: Ellipse
topics.description: The set of points whose sum of distances to two foci is constant; stretched circle.

topics.title: Isometry
topics.description: A transformation that preserves distances, hence congruence of figures (rigid motion).

topics.title: Translation (geometry)
topics.description: A transformation that slides every point of a figure the same distance in the same direction.

topics.title: Rotation (mathematics)
topics.description: A transformation that turns a figure around a fixed center point by a given angle.

topics.title: Reflection (mathematics)
topics.description: A transformation that flips a figure over a line (mirror line), producing a congruent image.

topics.title: Symmetry group
topics.description: The set of all isometries (including rotations, reflections) that map a figure onto itself.

topics.title: Congruence transformation
topics.description: Transformations (isometries) that map a figure to a congruent copy; used to prove congruence.

topics.title: Projective geometry
topics.description: A geometry that studies properties invariant under projection, unifying perspective and vanishing points.

topics.title: Projective plane
topics.description: An extension of the Euclidean plane where every two lines meet at exactly one point (no parallels).

topics.title: Non-Euclidean geometry
topics.description: Geometries in which the parallel postulate fails, including hyperbolic and elliptic systems.

topics.title: Hyperbolic geometry
topics.description: A non-Euclidean geometry where through a point not on a line there are infinitely many parallels.

topics.title: Elliptic geometry
topics.description: A non-Euclidean geometry with no parallel lines; all lines intersect, modeled on a sphere.

topics.title: Spherical geometry
topics.description: Geometry on the surface of a sphere where 'lines' are great circles; elliptic in nature.
